items.name: Dice set
items.description: This item encompasses a wide range of game pieces, including dice and decks of cards (for games such as Three-Dragon Ante). A few common examples appear on the Tools table, but other kinds of gaming sets exist. If you are proficient with a gaming set, you can add your proficiency bonus to ability checks you make to play a game with that set. Each type of gaming set requires a separate proficiency.

items.name: Pouch
items.description: A cloth or leather pouch can hold up to 20 sling bullets or 50 blowgun needles, among other things. A compartmentalized pouch for holding spell components is called a component pouch (described earlier in this section).

traits: I'm full of inspiring and cautionary tales from my military experience relevant to almost every combat situation.
I'm haunted by memories of war. I can't get the images of violence out of my mind.
I've lost too many friends, and I'm slow to make new ones.
I can stare down a hell hound without flinching.
I enjoy being strong and like breaking things.
I have a crude sense of humor.
I face problems head-on. A simple, direct solution is the best path to success.
ideals: Greater Good. Our lot is to lay down our lives in defense of others. (Good)
Responsibility. I do what must be done and follow orders, even if I don't like them. (Lawful)
Independence. When people follow orders blindly, they embrace a kind of tyranny. (Chaotic)
Might. In life as in war, the stronger force prevails. (Evil)
Live and Let Live. Ideals aren't worth killing over or going to war for. (Neutral)
Nation. My city, nation, or people are all that matter. (Any)
bonds: I would still lay down my life for the people I served with.
Someone saved my life on the battlefield. To this day, I will never leave a friend behind.
I'll never forget the crushing defeat my company suffered or the enemies who dealt it.
Those who fight beside me are those worth dying for.
I fight for those who cannot fight for themselves.
flaws: The monstrous enemy we faced in battle still leaves me quivering with fear.
I have little respect for anyone who is not a proven warrior.
I made a terrible mistake in battle that cost many lives, and I would do anything to keep that mistake secret.
My hatred of my enemies is blind and unreasoning.
I obey the law, even if the law causes misery.
I'd rather eat my armor than admit when I'm wrong.

activeQuests.title: The First Step
activeQuests.description: You awake in a strange clearing. The air is fresh, but the silence is heavy. You must get your bearings.

objectives.description: Master the Booklet: View all pages (Character, Map, Quests, Equipment, Codex)
Study Your Gear: Examine an item in your inventory
Begin the Journey: Move to a neighboring hex

storySummary: started their journey as a Human Fighter with the Soldier background.

conversationHistory.content:
The world comes into focus with a soft, golden wash of early morning light, the sun just beginning to push its way over the horizon. You lie on a sea of tall, wavering grasses that stretch as far as the eye can see, the plains shimmering faintly with dew. A distant rumble of thunder rolls across the sky, low and foreboding, as if some storm gathers far beyond the visible horizon. The air carries a faint, metallic scent, and a cool breeze whistles through the grass, tugging at your hair and the simple tunic you wear.

Around you, the landscape is a patchwork of untamed wilderness. To the south, the edge of a dense jungle looms, its canopy a dark, breathing wall of green. Northward rise rolling hills, their slopes dotted with scattered shrubs and the occasional wind‑blown stone. To the southeast, another stretch of that same impenetrable jungle beckons, while to the southwest the plains continue unbroken. The northeast mirrors the hills, and the northwest returns to open grassland. Far on the horizon to the north, the silhouette of a mountain range cuts a stark line against the sky, while far south the jungle spreads like a living sea, and distant hills rise in the northwest and north‑west.

Your mind is a blank slate—no memory of how you arrived, no recollection of a purpose beyond the name that echoes in your thoughts: TestHero. The rumble of thunder seems to carry a whisper of something unseen, a reminder that you are not alone in this vast expanse.

What will you do?
- **Survey your surroundings**: Scan the plains, hills, and jungle for any sign of a path, shelter, or something out of the ordinary.
- **Listen closely**: Focus on the distant thunder and the sounds of the wind, the grasses, and any hidden wildlife that might hint at nearby activity.
- **Choose a direction**: Move toward the hills to the north for higher ground, or head into the jungle to the south to seek cover, or continue across the open plains to see what lies ahead.

The world feels both wondrous and unsettling, as if the very land holds secrets waiting to be uncovered.

codexEntries.title: The Veiled Terror of the Whispering Vale
codexEntries.content:
**Physical Description**
The NonExistentMonster is a creature of paradoxical form, described by witnesses as a shifting silhouette of ebony mist interlaced with faint, phosphorescent veins that pulse like distant stars. Its true size is said to fluctuate, ranging from the stature of a horse to the breadth of a small hill, depending on the observer's fear. The entity possesses no discernible limbs; instead, tendrils of dark ether ripple outward, capable of grasping, slicing, or simply enveloping anything they touch. Its "head"—if it can be called that—is a roiling vortex of void, from which a low, resonant hum emanates, reminiscent of wind through cavernous catacombs.

**Biological Speculation**
Scholars hypothesize that the NonExistentMonster is not a flesh‑bound beast but a manifestation of raw magical entropy. Its "veins" appear to be conduits of ley‑line energy, allowing it to siphon ambient arcane power to sustain its ever‑changing mass. Some arcane theorists propose that it is a sentient echo of a forgotten primordial deity, trapped between planes and forced to feed on the fear and imagination of mortal minds. Its lack of conventional organs suggests a semi‑corporeal lattice, where thought and will shape physicality.

**Ecology & Habitat**
Legends place the creature deep within the Whispering Vale, a mist‑shrouded valley where the veil between worlds thins. Here, the air is heavy with lingering spells, and the ground is strewn with ancient runes that seem to pulse in rhythm with the monster's hum. The NonExistentMonster is rumored to hunt not for sustenance but for stories—absorbing the memories of those it ensnares, weaving them into its ever‑expanding tapestry of existence. It is said to appear most often during lunar eclipses, when the veil is at its weakest.

**Myths & Legends**
Travelers speak of the "Silence of the Vale," a phenomenon where sound itself is muffled, attributed to the creature's presence. Folk songs warn of the "Veiled Terror" that steals voices, leaving victims mute but eternally aware of their own thoughts. Some claim that those who survive an encounter gain a fragment of the monster's knowledge, at the cost of a lingering shadow that follows them for the rest of their days.

**Survivor's Tale**
"I thought I was chasing a wisp of fog, but the air grew cold and the world dimmed. When the darkness coalesced, I felt my mind being pulled like a thread. I screamed—though no sound left my throat—as tendrils of night wrapped around my arms. I broke free by shouting the name of my mother, a word of love that the void could not devour. I escaped the vale, but every night I hear that low hum, a reminder that the thing still watches, waiting for the next story to claim."
—Eldric Thorn, Ranger of the Silver Marches

codexEntries.title: Goblin – The Cunning Cinder of the Wilds
codexEntries.content:
**Physical Description:**
Goblins stand a scant three to four feet tall, their wiry frames cloaked in mottled hide and scavenged scrap‑cloth. Their skin, ranging from sallow green to a bruised brown, is thick enough to deflect a glancing blade yet supple for rapid, erratic motion. Prominent, pointed ears swivel like antennae, catching the faintest rustle of prey. Eyes burn amber or jaundiced yellow, granting them keen vision in dim twilight. A set of sharp, uneven teeth crowns a mouth that rarely smiles without intent.

**Physiology & Biology:**
Goblin anatomy betrays a life of constant foraging. Their skeletal structure is light yet reinforced by a lattice of cartilage that absorbs shock, allowing them to sprint up steep inclines and tumble through dense underbrush unscathed. Metabolic rates are unusually high; a goblin can subsist on a single morsel of meat or root for days, but prolonged famine drives them to cannibalism. Their digestive tract is adapted to break down both carrion and toxic fungi, a trait that fuels the infamous “glowing gut” rumor—when a goblin ingests phosphorescent spores, its belly emits a faint, eerie luminescence.

**Ecology & Habitat:**
These creatures favor the fringe of forests, craggy hills, and abandoned ruins where they can exploit the shadows. Goblin tribes—often called “clans” or “hordes”—establish sprawling tunnel networks, complete with crude traps, false floors, and hidden caches of pilfered goods. Their social structure is hierarchical, led by the strongest or most cunning “chief,” who commands through intimidation and the promise of loot. Seasonal migrations are rare; instead, goblins adapt their raiding patterns to the harvest cycles of neighboring settlements, striking when granaries are full and defenses are lax.

**Behavior & Tactics:**
While commonly dismissed as mindless brutes, goblins display a surprising degree of guile. They favor ambush, using the terrain to funnel prey into kill zones laced with pit traps, swinging logs, or crude snares. In group combat, they employ “hit‑and‑run” tactics: a volley of javelins or slings, a swift retreat into cover, then a sudden flanking surge. Their vocalizations—high‑pitched shrieks, guttural snarls, and mock battle cries—serve both to intimidate foes and coordinate attacks.

**Myths & Legends:**
Folklore paints goblins as the cursed offspring of the earth itself, birthed from the restless stones of forgotten catacombs. Tales speak of a “Goblin King” who commands the subterranean realms, wielding a crown forged from rusted iron and the teeth of dragons. Some bards claim that a goblin’s blood, when mixed with nightshade, can brew a potion of invisibility—though no alchemist has ever produced such a draught. In the northern highlands, it is whispered that goblins guard ancient runestones, stealing away any who dare read the inscriptions.

**Adventure Hook – A Survivor’s Tale:**
*“I thought the night would swallow me, but the goblin’s shriek tore the darkness apart. Its eyes glowed like embers, and for a heartbeat I saw the world through its savage mind—every crumbling stone a weapon, every rusted blade a promise. I fled with my life, but the echo of its laughter still haunts my dreams. Beware the shadows where the ground seems too soft; they are the goblin’s hand, reaching for any light you dare carry.”* – Aric Veldrin, ranger of the Silverwood.
